summary.data_source: NCDOT non-motorist crash data, Durham County (2019-2024)

racial_baseline.description: Pre-AI baseline: existing racial disparity in who bears crash risk in Durham, before any AI allocation decisions
racial_baseline.source: NCDOT non-motorist crash records 2019-2024 + Census ACS 2024

findings: Only 29% of Q1 high-risk tracts are detectable from the crash data vs 67% in Q5 — a 38pp structural detection gap
Ridge regression trained on real 2019-2023 non-motorist crash data with demographic features
The crash data shows systematic underdetection in the poorest quintile — a property of the training data's structure, not this specific model
This 38pp gap means any AI tool trained on Durham's crash data inherits a structural disadvantage in Q1 before the vendor makes a single design choice
